description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, speckled stone material that makes up the rest of the block. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, with a uniform dark coloration, suggesting a dense and heavy construction. The top surface appears to be made of a granite-like stone, with a rough, matte finish and visible mineral flecks. The grooves may serve a functional purpose, such as drainage or anti-slip, making the block suitable for urban environments. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the block.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its speckled stone top and solid dark sides, likely used as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped, grooved top surface and the solid, dark front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and regularity of the grooves, with the stone's speckled texture visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the full width of the grooved top and the solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the parallel grooves and the block's substantial thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in place on a level concrete or paved surface
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering or erosion; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
